prediction.rationale: Scaling usually helps logistic regression on tabular numeric features.

risks: overfitting or split variance if depth is unconstrained
prediction.rationale: Tree ensembles test a different inductive bias, but may not beat a strong scaled linear model on small tabular datasets.
prediction.action_specific_signal: Use random forest to test whether nonlinear interactions improve over the linear baseline.
prediction.claim_risk: A single tree-ensemble score should be treated as observation until checked across seeds.

prediction.rationale: Scaled SVC can be strong on small numeric classification tasks but needs careful comparison.
prediction.action_specific_signal: SVC tests a margin-based nonlinear hypothesis after linear/tree baselines.
prediction.claim_risk: SVC should not be declared best without repeated-seed evidence.

risks: can overfit with too many estimators
prediction.rationale: Boosting is a useful late comparison when linear and bagged-tree baselines are known.
prediction.action_specific_signal: Use boosting to test a staged-tree alternative under the remaining budget.
prediction.claim_risk: Do not report a robust winner from one boosted run.
